canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Clarity is most commonly defined as the quality of a model's output being understandable, well-structured, and free from ambiguity. The most frequent definition, appearing in seven papers, frames it as the extent to which an output is "unambiguous and clearly formulated, enabling precise interpretation." While this core idea is prevalent, some papers introduce additional facets; for instance, one study includes "linguistically fluent," and another extends the concept to encompass the "layout of the presented information" in addition to language. A smaller set of sources offer more specific framings, such as defining clarity for moderation comments as using "plain, simple, and clear language" to avoid overwhelming the user. The construct is operationalized in the literature primarily through human evaluation, with several papers reporting the use of multi-point rating scales to assess it. For example, one paper evaluates clarity for generated questions by asking annotators, "Is the question clear? (not ambiguous)" ("Achieving Human Parity in Content-Grounded Datasets Generation"). Clarity is frequently assessed as one of several subjective quality dimensions, often studied alongside accuracy, relevance, conciseness, and adherence to instructions.
papers: A Training-Free Length Extrapolation Approach forLLMs: Greedy Attention Logit Interpolation
BTC-SAM: LeveragingLLMs for Generation of Bias Test Cases for Sentiment Analysis Models
FIRE: Flexible Integration of Data Quality Ratings for Effective Pretraining
SafeKey: Amplifying Aha-Moment Insights for Safety Reasoning
Unmasking Deceptive Visuals: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models on Misleading Chart Question Answering
iKnow-audio: Integrating Knowledge Graphs with Audio-Language Models
Achieving Human Parity in Content-Grounded Datasets Generation
PandaLM: An Automatic Evaluation Benchmark for LLM Instruction Tuning Optimization
ALERT: AnLLM-powered Benchmark for Automatic Evaluation of Recommendation Explanations
CodeSCM: Causal Analysis for Multi-Modal Code Generation
MultiChartQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Multi-Chart Problems
Waste Not, Want Not; RecycledGumbel Noise Improves Consistency in Natural Language Generation

definitions.text: The extent to which a generated question is unambiguous and clearly formulated, enabling precise interpretation.
definitions.paper: Achieving Human Parity in Content-Grounded Datasets Generation

definitions.text: The latent quality of a model's generated text being easy to understand, coherent, and well-structured.
